title: Dungeon Disguise: Sunglasses Stealth Run
description: A dungeon warrior patrols a dark arena. The player must collect enchanted sunglasses scattered across the dungeon floor, then equip them onto the warrior to unlock a secret disco transformation. Each pair of sunglasses requires solving a placement puzzle by dragging it to the correct attachment point on the warrior's head while avoiding detection zones.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap with ES modules from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a dungeon stealth-disguise mini-game. Load 'shared_assets/dungeon_warkarma.glb' as the dungeon warrior character placed at the center of a dark stone-floored arena. Load 'shared_assets/SunglassesKhronos.glb' and clone it 5 times, scattering the sunglasses copies at random positions on the ground plane within a 10-unit radius circle around the warrior. Setup: Use a PerspectiveCamera at position (0, 8, 12) looking at origin. Add dim ambient light (0x334455, intensity 0.3) and a spotlight (0xffaa44, intensity 2) casting shadows from above. Render a circular ground plane (radius 12) with dark stone-like MeshStandardMaterial. Add a subtle fog (0x111122). Interactions (6-8 steps with validation): 1) Player clicks a sunglasses object on the ground to pick it up (raycasting validation: must click within 0.5 units of a sunglasses mesh). 2) The picked sunglasses attach to the cursor via mousemove, floating at y=3. 3) Player must drag and drop the sunglasses onto the warrior's head region (validate drop position is within a bounding box around the warrior's head, approximately y > 70% of warrior height and within x/z range of 0.5 units from center). 4) If dropped outside the valid zone, sunglasses fall back to their original ground position with a bounce tween. 5) Successfully placed sunglasses snap onto the warrior's head with a golden particle burst effect (use Points geometry with randomized velocities). 6) After equipping 3 sunglasses, trigger a disco transformation: swap the spotlight to a cycling RGB color animation, make the warrior slowly rotate, and spawn a radial pulsing glow ring on the ground. 7) After all 5 are equipped, display a 'DISGUISE COMPLETE' HTML overlay with a neon glow CSS animation. Add an on-screen counter showing 'Sunglasses: 0/5'. Validate each interaction step: prevent picking up a new pair while one is held, reject invalid drop zones with a red flash effect on the ground, and ensure sunglasses stack visually on the head with slight random rotation offsets. Use OrbitControls for camera but disable during drag operations.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  sunglassesCollected: number,         // count of sunglasses picked up from ground (0-5)
  sunglassesEquipped: number,         // count of sunglasses successfully placed on warrior head (0-5)
  currentlyHoldingSunglasses: boolean,         // true if player is dragging a sunglasses object
  warriorHeadY: number,         // y-coordinate of warrior head attachment point for validation
  discoModeActive: boolean,         // true when all 5 sunglasses equipped and transformation triggered
  totalSunglassesSpawned: number,         // initial count of sunglasses clones placed in scene (should be 5)
  lastDropSuccess: boolean,         // true if most recent drop attempt was valid placement
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance from camera to origin point in units
  spotlightIntensity: number,         // current intensity of overhead spotlight
  groundRadius: number,         // radius of circular ground plane in units
}
